blocks.content: Executing Agent Workloads in CI/CD

To scale agentic engineering, agent loops must run unattended in continuous integration pipelines. A headless run executes without user interaction, reading task instructions, running files, performing checks, and reporting results. This requires configuring non-interactive authorization, defining timeout thresholds, and capturing build artifacts.

Because headless pipelines cannot request human-in-the-loop approvals, any CLI invocation must include the --auto-approve flag or a custom workspace rule file (.gemini.md) specifying permitted actions. Ensure strict repository branch protections are active so agents cannot run unreviewed code changes.
Pre-Authenticating and Injecting OAuth Tokens

Since headless execution environments cannot prompt for interactive browser sign-ins, you must pre-authenticate the Workspace MCP servers on a secure workstation. Extract the generated token cache files, encode them, and inject them into the target container environment at runtime.

# On your secure workstation, run /mcp to authenticate all servers.
# Locate the generated credentials directory (usually ~/.config/antigravity/tokens)
# Package the credentials into a base64 string
tar -czf - -C ~/.config/antigravity/tokens . | base64 -w 0 > gws-tokens-base64.txt

# Save the contents of gws-tokens-base64.txt as a protected CI/CD secret variable: GWS_AUTH_TOKENS_BASE64
Production-Grade GitLab CI/CD Configuration
The following GitLab CI/CD snippet runs a headless agent sequence inside an ephemeral agy-box workspace, processing a pull request, querying Google Workspace, and generating a report.

stages:
  - analyze

run_headless_agent:
  stage: analyze
  image: private-registry.corp.internal/agy/agy-box:latest
  variables:
    AGY_WORKSPACE_DIR: "$CI_PROJECT_DIR"
    AGY_HEADLESS: "true"
    AGY_API_KEY: "$AGY_PROD_API_KEY"
  before_script:
    # Setup isolated temporary directory within workspace
    mkdir -p $CI_PROJECT_DIR/.tmp
    export TMPDIR=$CI_PROJECT_DIR/.tmp
    
    # Restore pre-authenticated Google Workspace tokens
    mkdir -p /home/agy_runner/.config/antigravity/tokens
    echo "$GWS_AUTH_TOKENS_BASE64" | base64 -d | tar -xzf - -C /home/agy_runner/.config/antigravity/tokens
    
    # Verify API authorization credentials
    agy --version
    agy auth verify --token $AGY_API_KEY
  script:
    # Execute the agent loop on the workspace.
    # --auto-approve allows executing commands without human prompts.
    # --timeout 600 limits execution time to 10 minutes.
    agy run \
      --task "Search Drive for 'release-notes-v2', read and summarize their contents, find the email for release-manager in Directory, and create a Gmail draft to them with the summary." \
      --auto-approve \
      --timeout 600 \
      --rules-file .gemini.md
  artifacts:
    name: agent-changes
    paths:
      - agy-report.json
    expire_in: 7 days
Advanced CI/CD Pipeline Automation (GitHub Actions)
GitHub Actions can also run headless agent operations. We use actions secrets to pass API keys securely and checkout only the specific pull request branch.

name: Headless Agent Automation
on:
  pull_request:
    paths:
      - 'src/**'

jobs:
  run-agent:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v3
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
          fetch-depth: 0
      - name: Restore Pre-Authenticated Workspace Tokens
        run: |
          mkdir -p $HOME/.config/antigravity/tokens
          echo "${{ secrets.GWS_AUTH_TOKENS_BASE64 }}" | base64 -d | tar -xzf - -C $HOME/.config/antigravity/tokens
      - name: Run Ephemeral AGY-Box Sandbox
        run: |
          docker run --rm \
            -v ${{ github.workspace }}:/workspace \
            -v $HOME/.config/antigravity/tokens:/home/agy_runner/.config/antigravity/tokens \
            -e AGY_API_KEY=${{ secrets.AGY_API_KEY }} \
            -e GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=/workspace/gcp-creds.json \
            private-registry.corp.internal/agy/agy-box:latest \
            agy run \
              --task "Check the Google Calendar for any event overlaps tomorrow, look for messages containing 'blocker' in Chat Space 'incident-response', and email the summaries to the team via Gmail." \
              --auto-approve \
              --workspace /workspace \
              --output-report /workspace/agy-report.json
      - name: Commit Agent Suggestions
        run: |
          git config --global user.name "AGY Headless Runner"
          git config --global user.email "agy-runner@corp.internal"
          git add src/
          git diff-index --quiet HEAD || git commit -m "style: automated agent security refactoring"
          git push origin HEAD:${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
Check Your Understanding
Question 1: What is the purpose of passing the GWS_AUTH_TOKENS_BASE64 secret into the headless container's config directory?
A) To bypass the corporate proxy checks.
B) To inject pre-authenticated Google Workspace OAuth tokens so the agent can execute tasks without interactive browser dialogs.
C) To authorize systemd to start Podman without root privileges.
D) To register the redirect URI callback endpoint.
Correct Answer: B. Restoring pre-authenticated token cache files bypasses the interactive login requirement, enabling headless pipelines to authenticate to Google Workspace MCP servers.
Question 2: Which flag must be provided to the agy CLI during a headless run to prevent the pipeline from hanging indefinitely?
A) --verbose
B) --auto-approve
C) --no-sandbox
D) --headless
Correct Answer: B. The --auto-approve flag tells the agent to proceed with command executions without waiting for user confirmation in the terminal.
